zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Lyster於1479年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾衛克菲德時，他的父親John Lyster30歲，母親Ann Beaumont25歲。他於1476年與Monica O’Hagen結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1552年3月21日在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lyster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Lister  .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Ljøsta in Romsdal, probably derived from Old Norse ljóstr ‘fishing spear, leister’.
Danish: nickname originally from Bornholm, derived from a word for a fishing tackle for catching eel.

variants.name: Lister
meanings: English: occupational name for a dyer of cloth, Middle English li(te)ster, a derivative of Middle English lit(t)en (Old Norse lita) ‘to dye’. This term was used principally in East Anglia and northern and eastern England (areas of Scandinavian settlement), and to this day the surname is found principally in these regions, especially in Yorkshire.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Lyster was born in 1479, in Wakefield, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Lyster, was 30 and his mother, Ann Beaumont, was 25. He married Monica O’Hagen in 1476. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 21 March 1552, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Lyster est né en 1479, à Wakefield, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Lyster, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Ann Beaumont, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Monica O’Hagen en 1476. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 21 mars 1552, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Lysterが1479年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアワケフィールドで生まれた時，父John Lysterは30歳，母Ann Beaumontは25歳であった。1476年にMonica O’Hagenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1552年3月21日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンで73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Lyster(이)가 1479년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 웨이크필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Lyster(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Beaumont(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1476년 Monica O’Hagen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1552년 3월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.
